findings: Species identity is supported: the plate matches the reference photos and live sources in its small slim form, pale blue-gray upperparts, grayish-white underparts, fine white eye ring, thin straight dark bill, long legs, and mostly black, white-edged cocked tail.
The illustrated bird shows one head, one beak, two legs, one tail, and two wings (the far wing is occluded by the lateral pose); legs, feet, bill, eye, and feather pattern are anatomically coherent.

All visible descriptive claims are supported by the live sources: family Polioptilidae; active foliage-foraging behavior including insects and spiders; wooded, scrubby, and often moist/near-water habitats; and the listed field marks.

Measurements are substantively correct. Audubon gives 10–11 cm, 16 cm, and 5–8 g; Cornell gives 10–11 cm, 16 cm, and 4.8–8.9 g. The plate's 3.9–4.4 in length combines the endpoints of the two authorities (Cornell gives 3.9–4.3 in; Audubon gives 4.0–4.4 in), a minor precision inconsistency but not a material text error.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.

verification_sources.title: Blue-gray Gnatcatcher Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
